target.title: fix(backup): resolve legacy hardlink targets by link subtree (follow-up to #89328)
target.body:
## Summary
Follow-up to #89328 (merged as 5be282e459), which still leaves the originally reported failure in place.

`backup verify` rejected previously valid backups containing internal hardlinks with `Archive hardlink target is outside the declared archive root` (#89257). Root cause: node-tar records a deduplicated hardlink's `linkpath` as the **cwd-relative source path** (e.g. `.openclaw/state/db`), while `onWriteEntry` only remaps the entry's own `path` into the `<root>/payload/posix/...` namespace.

#89328 reconstructs the target as `path.posix.join(archiveRoot, linkpath)` — that only re-adds the root segment, producing `<root>/.openclaw/state/db`. The real entry is `<root>/payload/posix/<abs-path>/.openclaw/state/db`, so a genuine `openclaw backup create` archive (linkpath `.openclaw/state/...`) **still fails verify on current `main`**.

This change resolves the target correctly. node-tar only dedupes files under a shared `cwd`, so the target is `<ancestor-of-link>/<linkpath>`: walk the link entry's own ancestor dirs and require an **exact** entry match.

- Fixes the real cwd-relative linkpath shape that `main` still rejects.
- Still accepts the root-relative shape #89328 targeted (its test is kept and passes here — when the walk reaches the archive-root segment, `join(root, linkpath)` is checked).
- Anchoring to the link's ancestor chain (instead of a global `endsWith` suffix scan) keeps the missing-target integrity check meaningful: a dropped/corrupt target can no longer be masked by an unrelated same-named file elsewhere in the archive.
- Absolute/backslash/`..` traversal rejection is unchanged (still in `normalizeArchivePath`); `verifyManifestAgainstEntries` still enforces every entry is under the archive root.

Out of scope (per the issue triage): the `backup create --verify` exit-13 / truncated `.tmp` half is a separate failure (Node unsettled top-level await abandoning the tar write) that needs live user state to reproduce. Not addressed here.

Refs #89257, #89328

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/backup-verify.test.ts src/infra/backup-create.test.ts src/commands/backup.create-verify.test.ts src/commands/backup.atomic.test.ts src/cli/program/register.backup.test.ts` → 18 passed
- `pnpm format:check` / `oxlint` / `pnpm tsgo:core` / `pnpm check:test-types` — clean
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior addressed:** `backup verify` now accepts older archives whose internal hardlink targets are recorded as node-tar cwd-relative paths, while still rejecting absolute, backslash, traversal, missing, and dropped-but-tail-colliding hardlink targets.

**Real environment tested:** Source checkout on this branch. Built a genuine archive with real `fs.link` + `tar.c` (default linkCache, dedup on) so the Link entry carries the actual cwd-relative `linkpath` `.openclaw/state/a.bin`; confirmed it failed under #89328's merged code and passes after this change. Also reproduced and fixed the suffix-scan over-acceptance with a dropped target plus an unrelated same-tail entry.

**Exact steps / commands run after this patch:**
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/backup-verify.test.ts` → 14 passed.
- Full backup test set (above) → 18 passed.

**Evidence after fix:** New regression tests — (1) exact legacy cwd-relative shape, (2) a real `fs.link`+`tar.c` deduplicated-hardlink archive end-to-end (asserts a `type:"Link"` entry with cwd-relative linkpath is actually produced, so coverage can't silently lapse), (3) a dropped target masked by a same-tail sibling must be rejected. #89328's root-relative test is retained and still passes. Existing unsafe/missing hardlink tests still reject.

**Observed result after fix:** `result.ok === true` for legacy internal-hardlink archives; traversal/absolute/missing/tail-collision targets throw.

**What was not tested:** Full `pnpm check && pnpm test`; the reporter's actual archive; the `backup create --verify` exit-13 temp-archive failure; applying to `release/2026.5.28`.
target.author: hoobnn
